decisions.title: Consolidate DLv3 operational observability before scaling
decisions.context: Multiple alerts (ENG-9058, ENG-9052, ENG-8961, ENG-9050) indicate fragmented monitoring. Need unified observability strategy before expanding DLv3 usage to more customers.

arguments.for: Prevents alert fatigue, reduces MTTR, builds confidence for Nail the Core pillar. Critical for production stability.
arguments.against: Delays feature work by ~2 sprints. Could monitor reactively instead.

decisions.title: Prioritize Family/Rules Polish over new Canvas features
decisions.context: ENG-8952, ENG-8953, ENG-8256, ENG-9055 show active polish work. Families/Rules are core to Decisions Captured metric. Canvas momentum may suffer.

arguments.for: Aligns with Nail the Core. Rules are decision capture mechanism. Polish increases adoption velocity.
arguments.against: Canvas differentiation delayed. Competitors may close gap on visualization capabilities.

decisions.title: Define data import strategy: HDF5/TDMS/Parquet priority order
decisions.context: Three parallel import efforts (ENG-8996 HDF5, ENG-8997 TDMS, ENG-8945 Parquet). Unclear which unlocks most customer decisions. Resource fragmentation risk.
decisions.blockedBy: Customer research on format usage distribution incomplete

arguments.for: Focuses engineering on highest-impact format. Faster time-to-value for import pillar.
arguments.against: Different customers need different formats. Parallel work maintains optionality.

decisions.title: Commit to kluctl deployment timeline or revert to current system
decisions.context: ENG-7716 for kluctl backend deployment in flight. No clear deadline. Deployment reliability is moat-building but risk if half-migrated.

arguments.for: Forces resolution. Either completes infrastructure moat or frees team for product work.
arguments.against: May pressure premature launch. Deployment infra shouldn't be rushed.

decisions.title: Establish Fresh Data Reads success criteria before memtable implementation
decisions.context: ENG-8225 defines criteria, ENG-8228 implements memtable reads. Criteria must be clear before building. Core performance differentiator.

arguments.for: Prevents building wrong thing. Success criteria drive architecture decisions. Memtable is moat-building tech.
arguments.against: Criteria may emerge from implementation. Parallel work saves time.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy decision-making week with 5 open strategic decisions. 73% meeting load indicates coordination overhead from parallel workstreams (DLv3 stability, imports, rules polish).
alerts: 3 consecutive days with <2 focus hours - decision quality may degrade
5 strategic decisions open simultaneously - prioritization needed
DLv3 operational issues consuming unplanned time - impacting roadmap
